data.title: How Titomic’s (ASX:TTT) European Leadership Refresh Could Shape Its Global Execution Story
data.description:
• Titomic Limited has announced leadership changes within its European division, with Chris Myers becoming President of Titomic Europe in early 2026 and Ferdy Touwen promoted to Deputy President & Head of European Operations, while outgoing leader Klaas Rozema moves into a Senior Executive Advisor role ahead of a planned sabbatical.
  • This reshaping of Titomic’s European leadership concentrates commercial and operational responsibility in a refreshed team, aiming to support execution, customer engagement, and the company’s broader global expansion efforts.
  • We will now examine how this refreshed European leadership structure, particularly Chris Myers’ expanded role, may influence Titomic’s overall investment narrative.

Uncover the next big thing with financially sound penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

Advertisement

What Is Titomic's Investment Narrative?

To own Titomic, you really have to believe that its cold spray and advanced manufacturing technology can scale commercially fast enough to justify a high A$9.43 million revenue base, ongoing A$19.89 million losses, and a very rich price to sales multiple. The big near term catalysts still sit around winning and executing sizable contracts in aerospace, defense, and industrial repair, and around proving that the newer management team can convert strong forecast growth into a path to profitability. The latest reshaping of European leadership fits into that story: it looks like a continuation of Titomic’s push to professionalize regional operations, rather than a thesis-changing event in itself, unless it meaningfully improves delivery from the new Heerenveen hub. The core risk remains execution against ambitious growth expectations while running an expensive, loss making business.

However, investors should be aware of how that rich valuation amplifies any execution slip. The valuation report we've compiled suggests that Titomic's current price could be inflated.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Six fair value views from the Simply Wall St Community span A$0.02 to A$5.00, so you are seeing very different expectations. Set against Titomic’s high price to sales ratio and ongoing losses, this spread underlines why understanding the execution risk around its new regional leadership structure really matters for anyone considering the stock’s future path.

Explore 6 other fair value estimates on Titomic - why the stock might be worth less than half the current price!

data.title: Does Deutsche Bank’s Wave of Long-Dated Senior Notes Signal a New Capital Playbook for (XTRA:DBK)?
data.description:
• In recent days, Deutsche Bank Aktiengesellschaft has issued and completed multiple fixed-rate, callable senior unsecured note offerings across US$-denominated Eurobond and Eurodollar formats, with maturities stretching from 2031 to 2051 and coupons between 4.30% and 5.75%.
  • These back-to-back issues, alongside a new head of private banking for Suisse Romande and a digital proxy-voting rollout, highlight an active funding and client-service agenda that could influence how investors assess Deutsche Bank’s balance sheet structure and capital allocation choices.
  • We’ll now examine how this wave of new senior unsecured note issuance might influence Deutsche Bank’s investment narrative and perceived capital strength.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 30 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Deutsche Bank Investment Narrative Recap

To own Deutsche Bank today, you need to be comfortable with a large, globally exposed lender that is still tightly watched for credit quality, litigation outcomes and capital resilience. This fresh wave of fixed rate, callable senior unsecured note issuance modestly reshapes the funding mix, but does not yet appear to alter the key near term swing factors of potential credit losses, especially in U.S. CRE, or the impact of any unexpected legal or regulatory costs.

Among the latest announcements, the series of US dollar Eurobond and Eurodollar senior unsecured notes maturing between 2031 and 2051 is most relevant, because it speaks directly to how Deutsche Bank is funding itself across the curve. For shareholders focused on catalysts like capital returns and balance sheet flexibility, this new long dated funding sits alongside existing dividend and buyback plans, and may influence how comfortably the bank can sustain those commitments through different conditions.

Yet while funding looks accessible, investors should still be aware of the risk that persistently elevated credit losses, particularly in U.S. commercial real estate, could...

data.title: Getting In Cheap On Edelweiss Financial Services Limited (NSE:EDELWEISS) Might Be Difficult
data.description:
There wouldn't be many who think Edelweiss Financial Services Limited's (NSE:EDELWEISS) price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 22.7x is worth a mention when the median P/E in India is similar at about 25x. Although, it's not wise to simply ignore the P/E without explanation as investors may be disregarding a distinct opportunity or a costly mistake.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

As an illustration, earnings have deteriorated at Edelweiss Financial Services over the last year, which is not ideal at all. One possibility is that the P/E is moderate because investors think the company might still do enough to be in line with the broader market in the near future. If you like the company, you'd at least be hoping this is the case so that you could potentially pick up some stock while it's not quite in favour.

View our latest analysis for Edelweiss Financial Services

Want the full picture on earnings, revenue and cash flow for the company? Then our free report on Edelweiss Financial Services will help you shine a light on its historical performance.
Advertisement

How Is Edelweiss Financial Services' Growth Trending?

In order to justify its P/E ratio, Edelweiss Financial Services would need to produce growth that's similar to the market.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered a frustrating 19% decrease to the company's bottom line. Still, the latest three year period has seen an excellent 100% overall rise in EPS, in spite of its unsatisfying short-term performance. Although it's been a bumpy ride, it's still fair to say the earnings growth recently has been more than adequate for the company.

It's interesting to note that the rest of the market is similarly expected to grow by 25% over the next year, which is fairly even with the company's recent medium-term annualised growth rates.

With this information, we can see why Edelweiss Financial Services is trading at a fairly similar P/E to the market. Apparently shareholders are comfortable to simply hold on assuming the company will continue keeping a low profile.

The Bottom Line On Edelweiss Financial Services' P/E

Typically, we'd caution against reading too much into price-to-earnings ratios when settling on investment decisions, though it can reveal plenty about what other market participants think about the company.

As we suspected, our examination of Edelweiss Financial Services revealed its three-year earnings trends are contributing to its P/E, given they look similar to current market expectations. Right now shareholders are comfortable with the P/E as they are quite confident future earnings won't throw up any surprises. Unless the recent medium-term conditions change, they will continue to support the share price at these levels.

Plus, you should also learn about these 2 warning signs we've spotted with Edelweiss Financial Services (including 1 which is significant).

If you're unsure about the strength of Edelweiss Financial Services' business, why not explore our interactive list of stocks with solid business fundamentals for some other companies you may have missed.

data.title: Aris Mining (TSX:ARIS) Is Up 6.7% After Segovia Reserve Upgrade And Soto Norte Control Shift
data.description:
• Aris Mining recently reported an updated Segovia mineral reserve and resource estimate effective November 28, 2025, showing increases in measured, indicated, inferred resources and reserves while maintaining high gold grades reviewed and approved under NI 43-101 standards.
  • Alongside this resource growth, the company has assumed full control of the Soto Norte project and is pursuing fully funded production expansion across Colombia and Guyana, underscoring its push toward a larger-scale gold business.
  • We'll examine how the Segovia reserve and resource increases reshape Aris Mining's investment narrative and future growth and risk profile.

To own Aris Mining, you need to believe it can turn its growing high grade resource base in Colombia and Guyana into sustained, profitable production while managing country and project execution risks. The latest Segovia reserve and resource upgrade supports the long term mine plan but does not materially change the near term focus, which still centers on delivering the current expansion projects on time and on budget without straining the balance sheet.

The most connected recent development is Aris Mining’s move to 100% ownership of the Soto Norte project, which, together with the Segovia update, reinforces the scale of its future project pipeline. This enlarges both the opportunity and the execution risk around its growth plans, particularly given its operational concentration in Colombia and the need to maintain supportive regulatory and community conditions as these projects advance.

data.title: Assessing Ashtrom Group (TASE:ASHG) Valuation After A Strong 1 Year Shareholder Return
data.description:
Ashtrom Group (TASE:ASHG) is back on some investors’ radar after recent trading moves, with the shares last closing at ₪78.17 and showing mixed short term returns alongside stronger performance figures over the past year.

See our latest analysis for Ashtrom Group.

The recent pullback, including a 1 day share price return of 1.04% decline and a 7 day share price return of 3.37% decline, sits against a stronger backdrop, with a 1 year total shareholder return of 31.45% indicating momentum that still looks constructive.

If Ashtrom’s move has you reassessing your watchlist, it could be a good moment to broaden your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Ashtrom trading at ₪78.17 and an intrinsic value estimate implying roughly an 8% premium, the key question is whether the recent 31.45% 1 year return leaves more upside or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Advertisement

Price to Earnings of 31.2x: Is it justified?

Ashtrom Group is trading on a P/E of 31.2x at a last close of ₪78.17, which places the shares on a richer earnings multiple than many construction peers but not at the very top of the range.

The P/E ratio compares the share price with earnings per share and is a common shortcut for how much investors are currently willing to pay for each unit of profit. For a construction and property business like Ashtrom, this multiple often reflects expectations around future project pipelines, asset utilisation and the stability of earnings through the cycle.

According to the data provided, Ashtrom's P/E of 31.2x is considered expensive versus the broader Asian Construction industry average of 14.8x. However, it is described as good value against a peer group average of 46x and is also below the Construction industry average of 33.2x. That mix of signals suggests the market is assigning a relatively high price to current earnings, while still leaving Ashtrom at a discount to more highly rated direct peers.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 31.2x (ABOUT RIGHT)

However, that premium P/E can quickly look exposed if project pipelines slow, or if earnings from property, construction or power operations come under pressure.

Find out about the key risks to this Ashtrom Group narrative.

Another View: Our DCF Estimate Looks Far Harsher

While the 31.2x P/E hints at investors paying up for Ashtrom Group, our DCF model presents a very different picture. At a last close of ₪78.17, Ashtrom is trading well above an estimated fair value of ₪8.56, which frames the current price as materially overvalued. It raises a simple question: are you comfortable paying almost ten times that fair value estimate?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Ashtrom Group for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 885 undervalued stocks based on their cash flows. data.title: Country Garden Holdings (SEHK:2007) Valuation Check After December 2025 Sales Update
data.description:
Advertisement

December sales update and why it matters for shareholders

Country Garden Holdings (SEHK:2007) has released preliminary unaudited sales figures for December 2025, reporting contracted sales attributable to shareholders of about RMB 2.69b and contracted sales gross floor area of roughly 0.31 million square meters.

For investors tracking this China focused developer, these monthly numbers provide another reference point alongside the company’s recent share price performance, multi year total returns and current earnings profile.

See our latest analysis for Country Garden Holdings.

The December sales update lands against a weak recent trading backdrop, with a 1 day share price return of 2.74% following a 30 day share price decline of 23.47% and a 3 year total shareholder return loss of 86.75%, which together suggest fading momentum and ongoing concerns about risk.

If this kind of volatility has you looking wider than a single Chinese developer, it could be a good moment to broaden your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With December sales at RMB 2.69b, a recent 23.47% 30-day share price decline, and a small 6.7% discount to the HK$0.40 analyst target, you have to ask: is there real upside here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Price-to-Sales of 0x, is it justified?

Based on the preferred multiple, Country Garden Holdings screens as very cheap on a sales basis, with the last close at HK$0.375 and a Price-to-Sales (P/S) ratio of 0x compared with both peers and an estimated fair level.

The P/S ratio compares the company’s market value to its revenue. It is often used for businesses that are unprofitable, as is the case here. For a China focused real estate developer with annual revenue of CN¥223,224.0m and a reported net loss of CN¥39,071.0m, sales based metrics can give investors an alternative lens when earnings are negative.

Country Garden Holdings is flagged as good value on this measure, with its 0x P/S ratio sitting well below the peer average of 0.5x in Hong Kong real estate and also below the broader Hong Kong Real Estate industry average of 0.7x. The estimated fair P/S ratio of 0.2x is still materially higher than the current level, which implies a gap the market could close if sentiment or fundamentals change.

data.description:

The latest quarter’s adjusted operating income beat but revenue and event volume miss, along with a modest share price pullback, does not materially alter that story in the near term, though it keeps attention squarely on Ticketmaster as the key risk.

Against this backdrop, Live Nation’s push into new markets, such as taking over Bangkok’s Impact Arena with planned upgrades and confirmed 2026 shows, is especially relevant. These kinds of venue and market expansions speak directly to the company’s core catalyst of growing its global concert footprint, but they also raise questions about execution risk and capital allocation discipline at a time when investors are already debating the balance between growth and profitability.

data.title: Assessing Sunstone Hotel Investors (SHO) Valuation After Baird Downgrade And Steady Dividend Yield
data.description:
Baird’s downgrade of Sunstone Hotel Investors (SHO) from Outperform to Neutral puts fresh attention on the REIT’s positioning versus peers, particularly given its consistent dividend history and current 3.82% yield.

See our latest analysis for Sunstone Hotel Investors.

The Baird downgrade comes after a mixed run for the stock, with a 1-day share price return of 1.08% and a 30-day share price return of 1.30%, set against a 1-year total shareholder return decline of 16.11%. Taken together, this suggests short-term momentum is trying to build while longer-term returns have been weaker as investors reassess Sunstone Hotel Investors’ risk and income profile.

If this kind of reset has you thinking about where else capital might work harder, it could be worth scanning fast growing stocks with high insider ownership as a starting point for fresh ideas.

With the shares trading at $9.35 against a $10.00 analyst target and an estimated intrinsic discount of about 37%, the key question is whether this signals mispricing or if the market already reflects Sunstone’s future prospects.

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 3% Undervalued

With Sunstone Hotel Investors last closing at $9.35 against a narrative fair value of $9.64, the current price sits just below that central estimate and puts the focus squarely on the earnings and cash flow assumptions behind it.

The normalization and recovery of key leisure markets such as Maui (Wailea/Kaanapali) and continued asset re-investment (for example, Andaz Miami Beach opening, renovations in San Antonio and San Diego) position the portfolio for outsized earnings growth as destination travel resumes and airlift increases, benefiting both top line and EBITDA.

Read the complete narrative.

Analysts are baking in steady revenue gains, fatter margins, and a richer profit base, all discounted at 8.53%. Curious which earnings and share count assumptions really drive that fair value?

Result: Fair Value of $9.64 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, the story can change quickly if high capital spending on renovations continues to squeeze free cash flow or if key markets like Maui and Washington, D.C. remain sluggish for longer.

Find out about the key risks to this Sunstone Hotel Investors narrative.

Another View: What The Sales Multiple Is Saying

While the narrative fair value suggests Sunstone Hotel Investors may be slightly undervalued, the current P/S ratio of 1.9x tells a more cautious story. It is higher than the peer average at 1.2x, yet sits below a fair ratio of 2.3x that the market could move toward over time.

In practice, that gap hints at limited room for error, since the stock already trades richer than peers even though recent returns, profit margins, and one off items have been challenging. The question for you is simple: do you think the business can grow into this pricing without more setbacks?

Cardinal Energy closed at CA$8.81 with a P/E of 18.5x, while our fair-value framework points to a lower earnings multiple and a higher DCF fair value.

The P/E ratio compares the share price with earnings per share, so a higher multiple usually means the market is paying more for each dollar of current earnings.

For Cardinal Energy, the SWS DCF model estimates a fair value of CA$23.64 per share, while the fair P/E ratio is cited at 12.9x versus the current 18.5x level. That combination suggests the share price is well below the modelled intrinsic value, yet the market is assigning a richer multiple than both the estimated fair ratio and peer benchmarks. This could reflect expectations around future earnings stability rather than aggressive growth.

Compared with the Canadian Oil and Gas industry average P/E of 14.7x and a peer average of 16.5x, Cardinal Energy screens as more expensive on earnings. The fair P/E ratio of 12.9x is materially lower than the current 18.5x, which points to a level the market could move toward if sentiment or earnings expectations change.

data.title: A Look At Five9 (FIVN) Valuation After Expanded Google Cloud AI Partnership
data.description:
Five9 (FIVN) is back in focus after expanding its partnership with Google Cloud, rolling out a joint Enterprise CX AI offering that embeds Gemini and Vertex AI into its contact center software.

See our latest analysis for Five9.

That partnership news lands against a weak recent trend, with a 30-day share price return of 10.88% and a 90-day share price return of 13.85%, while the 1-year total shareholder return of 49.71% and 5-year total shareholder return of 88.91% indicate longer term momentum has faded.

If this kind of AI driven customer experience story interests you, it could be worth scanning other high growth tech and AI stocks that are working on similar themes.

With the share price down over the past year, despite reported revenue of US$1,127.466m and net income of US$31.28m, plus an analyst price target well above the last close, you have to ask: is there real upside here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 44.3% Undervalued

The most followed narrative sees Five9’s fair value at US$33.81 per share, which is well above the last close of US$18.84, creating a wide valuation gap that hinges on how its AI and CX platform story plays out over time.

Five9's accelerated adoption of AI-driven solutions, highlighted by 42% Enterprise AI revenue growth and a surge in AI bookings (representing over 20% of Enterprise new ACV), positions the company to benefit from increasing enterprise investment in AI and automation for customer experience, supporting higher recurring revenues and expanded net margins as AI products command premium pricing.

data.title: Does Arcturus Therapeutics (ARCT) JPM Conference Spotlight Clarify Its Long-Term Platform Credibility?
data.description:

The J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference slot is helpful for visibility and potential partnerships, but by itself it is unlikely to change the core near term catalysts, which still center on clinical data progress, funding clarity and resolving any lingering concerns from delayed SEC filings. Recent share price swings and removal from the S&P Biotechnology Select Industry Index underline how sentiment can flip quickly when confidence in execution or reporting weakens. data.title: Is It Time To Reconsider Daiichi Sankyo Company (TSE:4568) After Recent Share Price Weakness?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Daiichi Sankyo Company’s current share price reflects its true worth, you are not alone. This article is designed to help you make sense of that question.
  • The stock last closed at ¥3,540, with returns of 2.6% over 7 days, 5.3% over 30 days, a modest 0.3% year to date, and a 13.1% decline over 1 year. The 3 year and 5 year returns sit at 9.7% and roughly 0% respectively.
  • Recent coverage of Daiichi Sankyo has focused on its position in the pharmaceuticals and biotech space and how investors are weighing that against its share price performance. This context helps frame whether the recent short term gains are a reset in sentiment or simply part of a longer period of mixed returns.
  • Daiichi Sankyo currently has a value score of 4/6, which means it screens as undervalued on four of six checks. Next, we will look at how different valuation approaches arrive at that view and why there may be an even better way to think about valuation later in the article.

Find out why Daiichi Sankyo Company's -13.1% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Daiichi Sankyo Company Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model looks at the cash Daiichi Sankyo Company is expected to generate in the future, and then discounts those amounts back to today to estimate what the business might be worth right now.

For Daiichi Sankyo, the latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of ¥61,413.21m. Analysts have provided explicit free cash flow estimates for several years ahead, with Simply Wall St extending those projections further out. By 2030, free cash flow is projected at ¥487,333.33m, and the ten year path between now and then is based on a mix of analyst forecasts and extrapolated estimates under a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model.

When these projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the DCF model produces an estimated intrinsic value of about ¥7,317.83 per share. Compared with the recent share price of ¥3,540, this implies the stock screens as 51.6% undervalued using this approach.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Daiichi Sankyo Company is undervalued by 51.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 885 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Daiichi Sankyo Company.

Approach 2: Daiichi Sankyo Company Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay for a share to the earnings that support that price. It helps you see how many years of current earnings the market is effectively pricing in.

Higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk often line up with a higher "normal" or "fair" P/E ratio, while lower growth expectations or higher risk usually justify a lower one. For Daiichi Sankyo, the current P/E is 23.41x. That sits above the Pharmaceuticals industry average of 16.48x and is very close to the peer average of 23.15x.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Daiichi Sankyo is 32.09x. This is a proprietary estimate of what P/E might be reasonable given the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks. It can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry because it adjusts for those company specific features rather than assuming all firms should trade on the same multiples.

Comparing the Fair Ratio of 32.09x with the actual P/E of 23.41x suggests the shares screen as undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1442 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Daiichi Sankyo Company Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, where you turn your view of Daiichi Sankyo Company into a clear story that links your assumptions about future revenue, earnings and margins to a financial forecast, then to a fair value, and finally to a simple comparison with today’s share price.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives are an easy tool that let you set your own fair value and expectations, then see how that stacks up against the current price so you can judge whether the stock looks attractive or stretched based on your view.

Because Narratives update automatically when new information such as earnings results or company news is added to the platform, your fair value view stays connected to the latest data without you needing to rebuild your analysis each time.

For Daiichi Sankyo, one investor might focus on the higher end analyst view using earnings of ¥669.1b and a price target of ¥6,800, while another might lean toward the lower end using earnings of ¥288.3b and a price target of ¥4,500, and Narratives simply make those different stories and fair values easy to compare side by side.

data.title: Does StepStone Group’s (STEP) Madrid Push Reveal a Deeper Shift in Its European Strategy?
data.description:
• In recent days, StepStone Group has reported leadership changes and expanded its European footprint by opening a Madrid office to serve clients in Spain, Portugal, and Andorra.
  • This combination of refreshed leadership and targeted European expansion highlights how the firm is positioning itself to deepen relationships across key private-markets hubs.
  • Next, we will examine how this European expansion shapes StepStone Group’s investment narrative for investors assessing its long-term positioning.

Explore 29 top quantum computing companies leading the revolution in next-gen technology and shaping the future with breakthroughs in quantum algorithms, superconducting qubits, and cutting-edge research.

Advertisement

What Is StepStone Group's Investment Narrative?

To own StepStone Group, you need to believe in its ability to convert a fast-growing, global private-markets platform into sustainable profitability, despite recent large losses and thin margins. The stock’s climb to an all-time high, alongside Barclays’ hold rating and modestly higher target price, suggests the market is already giving some credit for that story while remaining measured. The Madrid office fits neatly with the firm’s recent push into Riyadh and Dublin, reinforcing distribution and fundraising reach rather than fundamentally changing the near-term earnings picture. The more meaningful short term catalysts still center on whether new products like the STPEX evergreen fund scale efficiently and whether the leadership transition in private equity keeps performance and client retention on track. Against that, persistent losses, insider selling and a dividend not covered by earnings remain front-of-mind risks.

But this momentum sits uncomfortably alongside ongoing losses and uncovered dividends that investors should not ignore. The valuation report we've compiled suggests that StepStone Group's current price could be inflated.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Only one Simply Wall St Community fair value estimate, at US$9.10, sits far below recent prices, underlining how differently individuals can view StepStone, especially when recent leadership changes and expansion do not yet resolve profitability concerns.

data.title: Under The Bonnet, Cerillion's (LON:CER) Returns Look Impressive
data.description:
There are a few key trends to look for if we want to identify the next multi-bagger. Amongst other things, we'll want to see two things; firstly, a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and secondly, an expansion in the company's amount of capital employed. This shows us that it's a compounding machine, able to continually reinvest its earnings back into the business and generate higher returns. And in light of that, the trends we're seeing at Cerillion's (LON:CER) look very promising so lets take a look.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)?

For those that aren't sure what ROCE is, it measures the amount of pre-tax profits a company can generate from the capital employed in its business. Analysts use this formula to calculate it for Cerillion:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.33 = UK£21m ÷ (UK£75m - UK£12m) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Thus, Cerillion has an ROCE of 33%. In absolute terms that's a great return and it's even better than the Software industry average of 12%.

Check out our latest analysis for Cerillion

In the above chart we have measured Cerillion's prior ROCE against its prior performance, but the future is arguably more important. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering Cerillion for free.

So How Is Cerillion's ROCE Trending?

The trends we've noticed at Cerillion are quite reassuring. The numbers show that in the last five years, the returns generated on capital employed have grown considerably to 33%. The amount of capital employed has increased too, by 194%. So we're very much inspired by what we're seeing at Cerillion thanks to its ability to profitably reinvest capital.

In another part of our analysis, we noticed that the company's ratio of current liabilities to total assets decreased to 16%, which broadly means the business is relying less on its suppliers or short-term creditors to fund its operations. Therefore we can rest assured that the growth in ROCE is a result of the business' fundamental improvements, rather than a cooking class featuring this company's books.

The Bottom Line On Cerillion's ROCE

In summary, it's great to see that Cerillion can compound returns by consistently reinvesting capital at increasing rates of return, because these are some of the key ingredients of those highly sought after multi-baggers. Since the stock has returned a staggering 298% to shareholders over the last five years, it looks like investors are recognizing these changes. Therefore, we think it would be worth your time to check if these trends are going to continue.

On a final note, we've found 1 warning sign for Cerillion that we think you should be aware of.

If you'd like to see other companies earning high returns, check out our free list of companies earning high returns with solid balance sheets here.

data.title: Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M. (TLV:LUMI) is favoured by institutional owners who hold 52% of the company
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • Significantly high institutional ownership implies Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M's stock price is sensitive to their trading actions
  • 47% of the business is held by the top 25 shareholders
  • Analyst forecasts along with ownership data serve to give a strong idea about prospects for a business

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

A look at the shareholders of Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M. (TLV:LUMI) can tell us which group is most powerful. The group holding the most number of shares in the company, around 52% to be precise, is institutions. That is, the group stands to benefit the most if the stock rises (or lose the most if there is a downturn).

Given the vast amount of money and research capacities at their disposal, institutional ownership tends to carry a lot of weight, especially with individual investors. Therefore, a good portion of institutional money invested in the company is usually a huge vote of confidence on its future.

Let's delve deeper into each type of owner of Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M, beginning with the chart below.

See our latest analysis for Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M

What Does The Institutional Ownership Tell Us About Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M?

Institutional investors commonly compare their own returns to the returns of a commonly followed index. So they generally do consider buying larger companies that are included in the relevant benchmark index.

Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M already has institutions on the share registry. Indeed, they own a respectable stake in the company. This can indicate that the company has a certain degree of credibility in the investment community. However, it is best to be wary of relying on the supposed validation that comes with institutional investors. They too, get it wrong sometimes. It is not uncommon to see a big share price drop if two large institutional investors try to sell out of a stock at the same time. So it is worth checking the past earnings trajectory of Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M, (below). Of course, keep in mind that there are other factors to consider, too.

Institutional investors own over 50% of the company, so together than can probably strongly influence board decisions. Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M is not owned by hedge funds. Our data shows that Phoenix Excellence Pension and Provident Ltd. is the largest shareholder with 5.5% of shares outstanding. With 5.2% and 5.1% of the shares outstanding respectively, Menora Mivtachim Provident Funds and Meitav Investment House Ltd are the second and third largest shareholders.

Our studies suggest that the top 25 shareholders collectively control less than half of the company's shares, meaning that the company's shares are widely disseminated and there is no dominant shareholder.

Researching institutional ownership is a good way to gauge and filter a stock's expected performance. The same can be achieved by studying analyst sentiments. Quite a few analysts cover the stock, so you could look into forecast growth quite easily.

Insider Ownership Of Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M

While the precise definition of an insider can be subjective, almost everyone considers board members to be insiders. Company management run the business, but the CEO will answer to the board, even if he or she is a member of it.

Insider ownership is positive when it signals leadership are thinking like the true owners of the company. However, high insider ownership can also give immense power to a small group within the company. This can be negative in some circumstances.

Our information suggests that Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M. insiders own under 1% of the company. As it is a large company, we'd only expect insiders to own a small percentage of it. But it's worth noting that they own ₪15m worth of shares. Arguably recent buying and selling is just as important to consider. You can click here to see if insiders have been buying or selling.

General Public Ownership

With a 48% ownership, the general public, mostly comprising of individual investors, have some degree of sway over Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M. While this size of ownership may not be enough to sway a policy decision in their favour, they can still make a collective impact on company policies.

Next Steps:

It's always worth thinking about the different groups who own shares in a company. But to understand Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M better, we need to consider many other factors. Case in point: We've spotted 1 warning sign for Bank Leumi le-Israel B.M you should be aware of.

Ultimately the future is most important. You can access this free report on analyst forecasts for the company.

NB: Figures in this article are calculated using data from the last twelve months, which refer to the 12-month period ending on the last date of the month the financial statement is dated. This may not be consistent with full year annual report figures.

data.title: Irish Continental Group (LON:ICGC) Is Doing The Right Things To Multiply Its Share Price
data.description:
To find a multi-bagger stock, what are the underlying trends we should look for in a business? Firstly, we'll want to see a proven return on capital employed (ROCE) that is increasing, and secondly, an expanding base of capital employed. If you see this, it typically means it's a company with a great business model and plenty of profitable reinvestment opportunities. Speaking of which, we noticed some great changes in Irish Continental Group's (LON:ICGC) returns on capital, so let's have a look.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Understanding Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)

Just to clarify if you're unsure, ROCE is a metric for evaluating how much pre-tax income (in percentage terms) a company earns on the capital invested in its business. To calculate this metric for Irish Continental Group, this is the formula:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.17 = €78m ÷ (€698m - €226m) (Based on the trailing twelve months to June 2025).

Therefore, Irish Continental Group has an ROCE of 17%. In absolute terms, that's a satisfactory return, but compared to the Shipping industry average of 7.4% it's much better.

See our latest analysis for Irish Continental Group

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Irish Continental Group compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering Irish Continental Group for free.

So How Is Irish Continental Group's ROCE Trending?

Irish Continental Group has not disappointed with their ROCE growth. Looking at the data, we can see that even though capital employed in the business has remained relatively flat, the ROCE generated has risen by 181% over the last five years. Basically the business is generating higher returns from the same amount of capital and that is proof that there are improvements in the company's efficiencies. It's worth looking deeper into this though because while it's great that the business is more efficient, it might also mean that going forward the areas to invest internally for the organic growth are lacking.

For the record though, there was a noticeable increase in the company's current liabilities over the period, so we would attribute some of the ROCE growth to that. The current liabilities has increased to 32% of total assets, so the business is now more funded by the likes of its suppliers or short-term creditors. It's worth keeping an eye on this because as the percentage of current liabilities to total assets increases, some aspects of risk also increase.

In Conclusion...

To bring it all together, Irish Continental Group has done well to increase the returns it's generating from its capital employed. And with a respectable 71% awarded to those who held the stock over the last five years, you could argue that these developments are starting to get the attention they deserve. Therefore, we think it would be worth your time to check if these trends are going to continue.

If you want to continue researching Irish Continental Group, you might be interested to know about the 2 warning signs that our analysis has discovered.

While Irish Continental Group isn't earning the highest return, check out this free list of companies that are earning high returns on equity with solid balance sheets.
